title: populus deltoides var. wislizeni
intro: Populus deltoides var. wislizeni, also known as the Rio Grande cottonwood or Fremont cottonwood, is a deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its broad, triangular leaves and cotton-like seeds. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Populus deltoides var. wislizeni include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, salves, and tinctures, with the inner bark being a common source for extraction.
